methods.void beginArray(): Consumes the next token from the JSON stream and asserts that it is the beginning of a new array.
methods.void beginObject(): Consumes the next token from the JSON stream and asserts that it is the beginning of a new object.
methods.void close(): Closes this JSON reader and the underlying Reader.
methods.void endArray(): Consumes the next token from the JSON stream and asserts that it is the end of the current array.
methods.void endObject(): Consumes the next token from the JSON stream and asserts that it is the end of the current object.
methods.boolean hasNext(): Returns true if the current array or object has another element.
methods.boolean isLenient(): Returns true if this parser is liberal in what it accepts.
methods.boolean nextBoolean(): Returns the boolean value of the next token, consuming it.
methods.double nextDouble(): Returns the double value of the next token, consuming it.
methods.int nextInt(): Returns the int value of the next token, consuming it.
methods.long nextLong(): Returns the long value of the next token, consuming it.
methods.String nextName(): Returns the next token, a property name, and consumes it.
methods.void nextNull(): Consumes the next token from the JSON stream and asserts that it is a literal null.
methods.String nextString(): Returns the string value of the next token, consuming it.
methods.JsonToken peek(): Returns the type of the next token without consuming it.
methods.void setLenient(boolean lenient): Configure this parser to be be liberal in what it accepts.
methods.String toString(): Returns a string representation of the object.
